events.title: California Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Bears baseball team hosts the North Carolina Tar Heels for an ACC conference game. Come cheer on the Golden Bears.

events.title: California Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Bears baseball team continues their series against the North Carolina Tar Heels. Don't miss the exciting action at home.

events.title: California Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Catch the final game of the series as Cal Baseball takes on the North Carolina Tar Heels at Evans Diamond.

events.title: California Lacrosse vs. Stonehill
events.description: The California women's lacrosse team hosts Stonehill in an exciting home game. Support the Golden Bears as they compete.

events.title: California Lacrosse vs. North Carolina
events.description: Watch the Cal women's lacrosse team face off against North Carolina at California Memorial Stadium.

events.title: California Lacrosse vs. Marist
events.description: The Cal women's lacrosse team plays a home game against Marist. Come and enjoy the collegiate lacrosse action.

events.title: California Lacrosse vs. Clemson
events.description: Catch the California women's lacrosse team as they host Clemson for a key conference matchup.

events.title: California Lacrosse vs. Virginia Tech
events.description: The Cal women's lacrosse team celebrates Senior Day with a home game against Virginia Tech.

events.title: California Women's Tennis vs. Virginia
events.description: The Cal Women's Tennis team takes on Virginia in a home match at the Hellman Tennis Complex.

events.title: California Women's Tennis vs. Virginia Tech
events.description: Join the Cal Women's Tennis team as they compete against Virginia Tech at their home courts.

events.title: California Women's Tennis vs. Stanford
events.description: The annual rivalry match as Cal Women's Tennis hosts Stanford. Specific time to be announced.

events.title: Cal Invitational (Track & Field)
events.description: The Cal Track & Field team hosts their outdoor opener, the Cal Invitational. Witness collegiate track and field action.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events (Track & Field)
events.description: Day one of the Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events hosted by Cal Track & Field. Athletes compete in various multi-discipline events.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events (Track & Field)
events.description: The second day of the Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events continues. Experience top collegiate track and field talent.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Invitational (Track & Field)
events.description: The Brutus Hamilton Invitational concludes with a full day of track and field events. This day also marks Senior Day for the team.

events.title: Cal Tri Meet (Women's Gymnastics)
events.description: The Cal Women's Gymnastics team hosts a tri-meet at Haas Pavilion. Witness incredible athletic performances.

events.title: Cal Quad Meet (Women's Gymnastics)
events.description: The Cal Women's Gymnastics team competes in a quad meet at Haas Pavilion. A fantastic display of strength and artistry.

events.title: California Men's Tennis vs. Saint Mary's
events.description: The Cal Men's Tennis team hosts Saint Mary's for a non-conference match at their home courts.

events.title: California Men's Tennis vs. Wake Forest
events.description: Join the Cal Men's Tennis team as they take on Wake Forest in a home game at Hellman Tennis Complex.

events.title: Controllable AI: Control Theory meets Artificial Intelligence
events.location: 430 Soda Hall (the Wozniak Lounge), UC Berkeley
events.description: Explore the intersection of control theory and artificial intelligence in this seminar hosted by the EECS department. The talk will discuss how control principles can enhance AI systems.

events.title: CS Seminar: Understanding the foundation model pipeline: From imitation to exploration
events.description: A computer science seminar focusing on the intricacies of the foundation model pipeline, from initial imitation to advanced exploration techniques.

events.title: Intelligent Design Automation for Heterogeneous Electronic-Photonic Integrated Systems
events.description: This seminar delves into intelligent design automation strategies for complex electronic-photonic integrated systems, crucial for next-generation computing.

events.title: Writing Complex Information into the Brain
events.description: An EECS seminar discussing methods and challenges in embedding and retrieving complex information within neural networks and AI models.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Florenta Teodoridis)
events.description: The Entrepreneurship and Innovation seminar series presents a talk by Florenta Teodoridis. Join for insights into innovation and business strategy.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Filippo Mezzanotti)
events.description: A presentation by Filippo Mezzanotti as part of the TIE Seminar series. Engage with cutting-edge research in entrepreneurship and innovation.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Geoffrey Borchhardt)
events.description: Geoffrey Borchhardt presents his work in the Entrepreneurship and Innovation seminar. Discover new perspectives on startup development.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Samuel Goldberg)
events.description: Samuel Goldberg shares his research in the ongoing TIE Seminar series. A great opportunity for academic and entrepreneurial networking.

events.title: UC Berkeley Grad Slam Competition
events.location: Banatao Auditorium, Sutardja Dai Hall, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Cheer on Berkeley graduate students as they showcase their research in a series of compelling three-minute talks at the 2026 Grad Slam.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: A weekly seminar featuring research presentations and discussions from visiting scholars and Berkeley academics on labor and employment issues.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: The IRLE hosts its weekly seminar, offering a platform for cutting-edge research presentations and lively discussions in labor studies.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: Another installment of the IRLE's weekly seminar series, delving into critical topics in labor and employment policy and research.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: Join the IRLE for their weekly seminar, providing valuable insights into current trends and challenges in the workforce.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: Engage with leading experts at the IRLE's weekly seminar, exploring innovative research and policy discussions.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: The IRLE continues its seminar series with presentations on critical issues affecting labor and society. All welcome.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: Discover the latest findings and engage in thoughtful dialogue at the IRLE's weekly visiting scholar seminar.

events.title: IRLE Weekly Visiting Scholar Seminar Series
events.organizer: Institute for Research on Labor and Employment
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (specific room TBD, implied IRLE)
events.description: Concluding the month, the IRLE hosts another engaging seminar as part of its ongoing weekly series.

events.title: UC LAUNCH Spring 2026 Demo Day
events.description: Witness the next wave of entrepreneurs shaping the future as UC LAUNCH startups pitch their innovative ideas at Demo Day.

events.title: Dean's Speaker Series: Julie Zhuo
events.location: Spieker Forum (N670), UC Berkeley Haas School of Business
events.description: Julie Zhuo, Co-Founder & CEO of Sundial and author of 'The Making of a Manager', shares her insights in this Dean's Speaker Series event.

events.title: Dean's Speaker Series: Jennifer Hyman
events.location: Spieker Forum (N670), UC Berkeley Haas School of Business
events.description: Jennifer Hyman, CEO of Rent the Runway, speaks as part of the prestigious Dean's Speaker Series at Berkeley Haas.

events.title: Career Counseling 1:1's Drop Ins
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (In Person, specific location TBD)
events.description: Graduate students can drop in for individual career counseling sessions to discuss career paths and job search strategies.

events.title: AIGP Presents: Spring Indigenous Grad Student Ceramic Workshop
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (In Person, specific location TBD)
events.description: An enriching ceramic workshop tailored for Indigenous graduate students, fostering creativity and community. All materials provided.

events.title: Loan Repayment + Real Life: Budgeting Around Debt
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (In Person, specific location TBD)
events.description: Learn essential budgeting strategies and loan repayment options to manage debt effectively after graduation.

events.title: Career Counseling 1:1's Drop Ins
events.location: UC Berkeley Campus (In Person, specific location TBD)
events.description: Another opportunity for graduate students to receive personalized career guidance and support for their professional development.

events.title: 2026 First Generation End of Year Celebration
events.location: The Inclusive Excellence Hub, 2515 Channing Way Berkeley, CA
events.description: Celebrate the achievements of first-generation graduate students, faculty, staff, and allies at this annual end-of-year gathering.

events.title: Refusing To Be a Weapon: Holocaust Memory and the Jewish Left in an Age of Genocide
events.description: A conversation with Marianne Hirsch and Benjamin Balthaser exploring the complexities of Holocaust memory and the Jewish Left.

events.title: Lu Liang: Geospatial Intelligence for Environmental Design | Public Land, Public Space, Public Discourse
events.description: Lu Liang presents on the application of geospatial intelligence in environmental design, focusing on public land and discourse.

events.title: Keynote Lecture: The Last Schools of Traditional Pacific Navigation
events.location: Jarvis Auditorium, Grimes Engineering Center, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Traditional Pacific navigators share their ancient knowledge systems and practices in this keynote lecture, fostering cultural exchange.

events.title: 2026 Matsui Lecture: Congressional Power in Turbulent Times
events.description: The annual Matsui Lecture addresses the evolving dynamics of congressional power in today's turbulent political landscape.

events.title: New Directions: Colonial Legacies, Post-Colonial Perspectives
events.description: A discussion on new approaches to understanding colonial legacies and developing post-colonial perspectives in various fields.

events.title: The Politicization of Maternal Mortality Data
events.description: This event examines how maternal mortality data is politicized and its implications for public health and policy.

events.title: Bate-Papo
events.description: A casual 'Bate-Papo' (chat) event, likely for students to practice language skills or engage in informal discussion.

events.title: What do the rich owe the rest of us?: A conversation on taxation and democracy
events.description: An engaging conversation exploring ethical and economic questions surrounding taxation, wealth distribution, and democratic principles.

events.title: Dorothee Wierling | The German Blues. A Historian's Perspective on the Rise of Right-Wing Populism in Eastern Germany
events.description: Historian Dorothee Wierling discusses the historical context and factors contributing to the rise of right-wing populism in Eastern Germany.

events.title: Sandip Roy | Chapal Rani, the Last Queen of Bengal: The Life and Times of a Female Impersonator
events.description: Sandip Roy presents on the fascinating life of Chapal Rani, a renowned female impersonator from Bengal, exploring cultural and historical contexts.

events.title: [Panel] Careers in Labor and Social Justice
events.description: A panel discussion offering insights into various career paths within the fields of labor advocacy and social justice.

events.title: Reporting on and Researching the Politics of Food and Farming: Journalist Mark Schapiro in conversation with 'Food Fix' founder Helena Bottemiller Evich
events.description: Journalist Mark Schapiro and 'Food Fix' founder Helena Bottemiller Evich discuss the intersection of journalism, research, and food politics.

events.title: Berkeley Book Chat with Andreja Novakovic
events.description: Join Andreja Novakovic for a casual book chat, offering an opportunity to discuss literature and connect with fellow book enthusiasts.

events.title: Inka Gastropolitics: stratified and stable
events.description: A presentation exploring the 'gastropolitics' of the Inka empire, examining food, power, and societal structures.

events.title: Curators' Tour: Margot Norton and Tausif Noor on Rhapsody: Works from the Cooper Rosenwasser Collection
events.description: Join curators Margot Norton and Tausif Noor for a guided tour and discussion of selected works from the Cooper Rosenwasser Collection.

events.title: Gerónimo Barrera de la Torre | Holding Space for Epistemic Multiplicity Through Participatory Audio and Visual Methods: A Guided Exploration of Method
events.description: Gerónimo Barrera de la Torre explores participatory audio and visual methods for embracing epistemic multiplicity in research.

events.title: Indigenous Research Methods Speaker Series: The Scale of Indigenous Peoples' Actions to Address Climate Change
events.description: This speaker series event highlights the significant and diverse actions Indigenous peoples are taking to combat climate change.

events.title: Abelardo de la Cruz | Prayer Specialists: The Devotions and Discourse of the Motiochihuanih as Religious Leaders in ...
events.description: Abelardo de la Cruz discusses the role of 'prayer specialists' and their spiritual leadership within indigenous communities.

events.title: Whatever You Want, Man. An Adaptation of Shakespeare's As You Like It
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: An imaginative adaptation of Shakespeare's 'As You Like It' where the Forest of Arden meets modern settings.

events.title: Whatever You Want, Man. An Adaptation of Shakespeare's As You Like It
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Experience another performance of this unique adaptation of Shakespeare's classic, interweaving old and new.

events.title: Re-Entry and Transfer Student Social
events.location: Game Zone, 1st Floor of MLK Jr. Building, UC Berkeley
events.description: A fun social event for re-entry and transfer students to connect, enjoy refreshments, and build community on campus.

events.title: DIY Shirt Patches Workshop
events.location: UC Berkeley Student Union (specific location TBD)
events.description: Unleash your creativity at this workshop where you can design and make your own unique DIY shirt patches. All materials provided.

events.title: St. Patrick's Day Celebration
events.location: UC Berkeley Student Union (specific location TBD)
events.description: Celebrate St. Patrick's Day with sweet treats, crafts, and festive activities hosted by the Student Union.

events.title: March Game Night!
events.description: Join Board Games at Berkeley and the Student Union for a night of fun games. Bring your own or try new ones, with snacks provided.

events.title: Graduate Student Patio Party (Afternoon Session)
events.description: An afternoon patio party for graduate students to socialize and enjoy tasty bites, in partnership with the Grad Division.

events.title: Graduate Student Patio Party (Evening Session)
events.description: Continue the evening's festivities at this patio party exclusively for UC Berkeley Graduate students, with refreshments and music.

events.title: Movie Night at Headlands - Legally Blonde
events.description: Enjoy a screening of the charming and empowering film 'Legally Blonde' at Headlands, hosted by the Student Union.

events.title: Commence Your Career: Connecting Seniors & Alums
events.description: An interactive event connecting graduating seniors with Cal alumni for career-building conversations and networking opportunities.

events.title: Ramadan Market and Crafts
events.location: UC Berkeley Student Union (specific location TBD)
events.description: Celebrate the end of Ramadan with a vibrant market featuring Muslim-inspired food, local vendors, and engaging crafts.

events.title: Noon Concert: Chamber Music
events.description: Students from the UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra perform small ensemble chamber works in this lunchtime concert.

events.title: Friday Concert: Faculty Recital (Stacey Pelinka, flute; Leighton Fong, cello)
events.description: A captivating faculty recital featuring Stacey Pelinka on flute and Leighton Fong on cello, performing various works.

events.title: Composition Colloquium: Pablo Teutli (Transmodernity in poetry and modernity in art)
events.location: Wu Performance Hall (Morrison Hall, Room 125), UC Berkeley
events.description: Pablo Teutli discusses themes of transmodernity in poetry and modernity in art in this composition colloquium.

events.title: UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra
events.description: The UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra presents a program featuring works by Keiko Abe, Prokofiev, and Strauss.

events.title: Composition Colloquium: Jenny Xiong (Seasons, Silence, and Art)
events.location: Wu Performance Hall (Morrison Hall, Room 125), UC Berkeley
events.description: Jenny Xiong explores the interplay of seasons, silence, and art in this insightful composition colloquium.

events.title: Music Studies Colloquium: Birgit Lodes (Echoes of Dedication: Beethoven and the Women Who Shaped His Art)
events.description: Birgit Lodes presents on Beethoven's relationships and the women who influenced his artistic development.

events.title: An Evening of Creative Jazz with the Berkeley Nu Jazz Collective and the Ben Goldberg/Myra Melford Group
events.description: An evening of innovative jazz performances by the Berkeley Nu Jazz Collective and the renowned Ben Goldberg/Myra Melford Group.

events.title: Opera Parallèle: La Belle et la Bête
events.description: Cal Performances presents Opera Parallèle's production of 'La Belle et la Bête' (Beauty and the Beast).

events.title: Opera Parallèle: La Belle et la Bête
events.description: A second opportunity to experience Opera Parallèle's enchanting interpretation of 'La Belle et la Bête'.

events.title: JACK Quartet
events.description: The critically acclaimed JACK Quartet performs a program of new and contemporary music. A must-see for chamber music enthusiasts.

events.title: Silkroad Ensemble with Rhiannon Giddens: Sanctuary: The Power of Resonance and Ritual
events.description: The Silkroad Ensemble, featuring Rhiannon Giddens, explores themes of sanctuary and ritual through a powerful musical performance.

events.title: Silkroad Ensemble with Rhiannon Giddens: Sanctuary: The Power of Resonance and Ritual
events.description: A second performance by the Silkroad Ensemble and Rhiannon Giddens, showcasing their unique blend of cultural traditions.

events.title: Drum Tao: The Best
events.description: Experience the dynamic and powerful drumming of Drum Tao in 'The Best,' a high-energy performance.

events.title: Drum Tao: The Best
events.description: Another chance to see Drum Tao's spectacular drumming and choreography at Zellerbach Hall.

events.title: Théotime Langlois de Swarte, violin; Justin Taylor, harpsichord
events.description: A captivating performance by violinist Théotime Langlois de Swarte and harpsichordist Justin Taylor, showcasing early music masterpieces.

events.title: David Finckel, cello; Wu Han, piano
events.description: The acclaimed duo of David Finckel (cello) and Wu Han (piano) perform a program of classical masterpieces.

events.title: Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater
events.description: The renowned Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater brings their powerful and inspiring performances to Berkeley.

events.title: Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater
events.description: Another opportunity to witness the extraordinary artistry and athleticism of the Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater.

events.title: Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater
events.description: Continuing their engagement, the Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater presents a diverse program of modern dance.

events.title: Cal Performances' 2026 Gala featuring The Joffrey Ballet
events.description: An annual gala event featuring a special performance by The Joffrey Ballet. A highlight of the Cal Performances season.

events.title: The Joffrey Ballet: Midsummer Night's Dream (West Coast Premiere)
events.description: The West Coast Premiere of The Joffrey Ballet's 'Midsummer Night's Dream,' a magical evening of dance.

events.title: The Joffrey Ballet: Midsummer Night's Dream
events.description: A stunning performance of The Joffrey Ballet's 'Midsummer Night's Dream,' showcasing their exquisite artistry.

events.title: The Joffrey Ballet: Midsummer Night's Dream
events.description: The final performance of The Joffrey Ballet's 'Midsummer Night's Dream' at Cal Performances.

events.title: Jordi Savall Hespèrion XXI La Capella Reial de Catalunya: Songs, Battles, and Dances from the Old and the New World (1100–1780)
events.description: Jordi Savall leads Hespèrion XXI in a journey through early music, exploring diverse sounds from across centuries and continents.

events.title: Alexandre Kantorow, piano
events.description: Pianist Alexandre Kantorow presents a solo recital, showcasing his virtuosity and musical interpretations.

events.title: The Tallis Scholars: Mysteries and Miracles
events.description: The renowned Tallis Scholars perform a program of sacred choral music, known for their pure and expressive sound.

events.title: Theatre of Voices
events.description: Theatre of Voices, led by Paul Hillier, presents a program of vocal music, ranging from early to contemporary works.

events.title: Víkingur Ólafsson, piano: Opus 109
events.description: Pianist Víkingur Ólafsson performs Beethoven's transformative Opus 109, a cornerstone of the piano repertoire.

events.title: Renée Fleming, soprano; Inon Barnatan, piano
events.description: The legendary soprano Renée Fleming joins forces with pianist Inon Barnatan for an exquisite recital.

events.title: University Chorus (Verdi, Requiem)
events.description: The UC Berkeley University Chorus performs Verdi's powerful Requiem, a monumental work of choral music.

events.title: University Chorus (Verdi, Requiem)
events.description: A second opportunity to hear the UC Berkeley University Chorus perform Verdi's profound Requiem.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: The Berkeley Dance Project showcases a vibrant array of dance works choreographed by students, faculty, and guest artists.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: A diverse program of dynamic duets and high-energy group dances from the Berkeley Dance Project.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: The Berkeley Dance Project continues with another evening of compelling and innovative dance performances.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: The final performance of the Berkeley Dance Project 2026, a celebration of dance and artistic expression.

events.title: 2026 Spring Nonprofit, Environment & Public Service Career Fair
events.description: Meet with organizations recruiting students for careers in the nonprofit, environmental, and public service sectors.

events.title: CRB Annual Community Gathering
events.location: Wells Fargo C420, Haas School of Business, UC Berkeley
events.description: An annual gathering to reconnect, network, and discuss CRB's initiatives and future collaborations in responsible business.
